rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees In The Amount Of $13,891.00 (Added To Calendar To Allow Full Text Of The Tentative Ruling To Be Displayed)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Trammell did not challenge these requested costs, and the court finds them to be reasonable. Trammell's claim that Keystone is entitled to fees only if the court finds Trammell's opposition "frivolous" is simply wrong on the law. A "frivolous" finding is required only when the court is contemplating awarding fees to a plaintiff who defeats an anti-SLAPP motion. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 425.16, subd. (c)(1) [second sentence].) Trammel's claim that Keystone's motion is improper because Trammell has appealed the anti-SLAPP ruling(s) is, again, wrong on the law. His authorities, section 404.5 of the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, rule 3.515, address stays pending a determination on coordination; coordination is not at issue in this case. Trammell is ordered to pay to Keystone $13,891 in attorney's fees incurred in connection with the (1) Notice of Special Motion and Special Motion to Strike (CCP section 425.16) and (2) Petitioner's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Special Motion to Strike (CCP section 425.16) Second Amended Petition Under Probate Code sections 850, 859, and $149 in costs incurred in filing the Notice of Special Motion and Special Motion to Strike (CCP section 425.16), for a total of $14,040. Keystone is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 29, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees In The Amount Of $13,891.00
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Pending before this court is Petitioner Donald Trammell's ("Trammell") Second Amended Petition Under Probate Code Section 850, 859 ("SAP"). Trammell is the beneficiary of the Estate at the center of this proceeding. He alleges that Lawrence Porchia wrongfully seized Estate real property known as 64 Potomac Street, sold it, and wrongfully distributed and/or withheld the proceeds. Trammell named Keystone Law Group as a respondent, alleging that Keystone breached a fiduciary duty owed to him as a beneficiary when it accepted $50,647.85 as payment for its litigation work for Porchia. He further alleged that Keystone's "actions are [sic] in bad faith." As relief, he requested that the court order Keystone to return the funds to the Estate and order Keystone to pay double damages, attorney's fees and costs based on a bad-faith finding. Keystone filed a special motion to strike under the anti-SLAPP statute, Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16. Trammell's argument and evidence in opposition made clear that his grievance against Keystone is based on Keystone's advocacy in this proceeding and the related trust proceeding (PTR-18-302129). The court granted Keystone's anti-SLAPP motion finding that Trammell's claim against Keystone arose from Keystone's protected activity and Trammell failed to show any likelihood of success on the merits. Subsequently, the court denied Trammell's motion for reconsideration. Now, Keystone moves for an award of $13,891 in attorney's fees and $149 in costs under subdivision (c)(1) of section 425.16. Keystone's motion is GRANTED. Under CCP section 425.16(c)(1), "Except as provided in paragraph (2), in any action subject to subdivision (b), a prevailing defendant on a special motion to strike shall be entitled to recover his or her attorney's fees and costs." The award of such fees and costs is mandatory under the statute when a defendant prevails on a CCP section 425.16 motion. (Ketchum v. Moses (2001) 24 Cal.4th 1122, 1141 ("Ketchum")). The burden of proving entitlement to the requested amount rests on the moving party. (Christian Research Institute v. Alnor (2008) 81 Cal.Rptr.3d 866, 874.) The amount of the award is within the discretion of the trial court, and the requested award amount "should be reduced if claimed hours are excessive, redundant, or otherwise unnecessary." (See Ketchum, supra, 24 Cal. 4th at p. 1131.) Keystone is a prevailing defendant on a special motion to strike. Thus, Keystone is entitled to fees and costs. The court has reviewed Keystone's motion and supporting evidence and finds fees of $13,891 reasonable and amply supported. Keystone's time records show that it has properly limited its fee request to the fees incurred in connection with the anti-SLAPP motion and subsequent related motion practice. (See, S.B. Beach Properties v. Berti (2006) 39 Cal.4th 374, 381; Lafayette Morehouse, Inc. v. Chronicle Publishing Co. (1995) 39 Cal.App.4th 1379, 1383.) The Memorandum of Costs worksheet filed with this Motion identifies $149 in costs incurred in filing and serving the anti-SLAPP motion. [Part 1 of 2]
